title: System and method for facilitating dynamic remapping of absolute addresses during software migration
abstract: A method includes receiving (501) at least one source code file (210) associated with a legacy hardware platform, the at least one source code file being part of a software migration from the legacy hardware platform to a target hardware platform (204). The method also includes identifying (505) one or more absolute memory addresses of the legacy hardware platform included in the at least one source code file. The method further includes for each of the one or more absolute memory addresses, including (509) an address remapping function (260) in an assembly language instruction block (306) associated with the target hardware platform.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method comprising: receiving (501 ) at least one source code file (210) associated with a legacy hardware platform, the at least one source code file being part of a software migration from the legacy hardware platform to a target hardware platform (204); identifying (505) one or more absolute memory addresses of the legacy hardware platform included in the at least one source code file; and for each of the one or more absolute memory addresses, including (509) an address remapping function (260) in an assembly language instruction block (306) associated with the target hardware platform. 2. The method of Claim 1 , further comprising: compiling (503) the at least one source code file into first assembly language instructions (230) executable in the legacy hardware platform; and translating (507) the first assembly language instructions into second assembly language instructions (250) executable in the target hardware platform. 3. The method of Cl aim 2, wherein each of the one or more absolute memory addresses of the legacy hardware platform is identified during the compiling by identifying a typecasting operator in the at least one source code file that includes the absolute memory address as an argument. 4. The method of Claim 3, wherein the assembly language instruction block includes the first assembly language instructions, and the address remapping function is included in the first assembly language instructions during the compiling in response to identifying the typecasting operator. 5. The method of Cl aim 2, wherein the assembly language instruction block includes the second assembly language instructions, and the address remapping function is included in the second assembly language instructions during the translating. 6. The method of Claim 1 , wherein the address remapping function includes 

 instructions to determine from a lookup table (270) a second memory address of the target hardware platform for each of the one or more absol ute memory addresses of the legacy hardware platform. 7. An apparatus comprising: at least one network interface (6.10) configured to receive and transmit data over at least one network; and at least one processing device (113, 123, 604) configured to: receive at least one source code file (210) associated with a legacy- hardware platform, the at least one source code file being part of a software migration from the legacy hardware platform to a target hardware platform (204); identify one or more absolute memory addresses of the legacy hardware platform included in the at least one source code file; and for each of the one or more absolute memory addresses, include an address remapping function (260) in an assembly language instruction block associated with the target hardware platform. 8. The apparatus of Claim 7, wherein the at least one processing device is further configured to: compile the at least one source code file into first assembly language instructions (230) executable in the legacy hardware platform; and translate the first assembly language instructions into second assembly language instructions (250) executable in the target hardware platform. 9. The apparatus of Claim 8, wherein the at least one processing device is configured to identify each of the one or more absolute memory addresses of the legacy hardware platform during the compiling by identifying a typecasting operator in the at least one source code file that includes the absolute memory address as an argument. 10. A non-transitory computer readable medium containing instructions that, when executed by at least one processing device ( 1 13, 123, 604), cause the at least one processing device to: 

 receive at least one source code file (210) associated with a legacy hardware platform, the at least one source code file being part of a software migration from the legacy hardware platform to a target hardware platform (204); identify one or more absolute memory addresses of the legacy hardware platform included in the at least one source code file; and for each of the one or more absolute memory addresses, include an address remapping function (260) in an assembly language instruction block associated with the target hardware platform.
